puzzle.title: The Perishable Paradise
puzzle.intro: Before you stands a soul who was granted immense blessings from Allah—lush gardens, flowing rivers, and abundant fruit. Yet, in the face of this prosperity, a test of faith and gratitude arose. This individual's response to their wealth led to a powerful lesson about the true nature of worldly possessions and the eternal power of Allah. Can you uncover the identity of this figure whose story is a timeless warning?
clues: I was blessed with more wealth and more followers than my companion.
I walked into my garden, wronging my own soul with my boastful attitude.
I declared that I believed my prosperity would never end, and I even expressed doubt about the Final Hour.
My righteous companion advised me to remember Allah upon entering my garden, saying 'Ma sha' Allah, la quwwata illa billah' (What Allah willed has occurred; there is no power except in Allah).
A calamity from the sky struck my property overnight, and all my bounty was ruined.
I was left wringing my hands in regret over all I had spent, as my garden now lay collapsed on its trellises, wishing I had never associated anyone with my Lord.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Owner of the Two Gardens
identity.answer: The Owner of the Two Gardens

trial.label: What Was My Primary Test?
options: Arrogance in the face of wealth
Being swallowed by the earth with my treasures
Building a golden calf for worship
Surviving a great flood on an ark
trial.answer: Arrogance in the face of wealth

location.label: What Was the Key Element in My Story?

outcome.label: What Was the Result of My Actions?
options: I was granted control over the wind
My wealth was given to the poor by my command
I was exiled to a remote island

puzzle.arabic: وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِۦ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّىٓ أَحَدًۭا
puzzle.verse: And his fruits were encompassed [by ruin], so he began to turn his hands about [in dismay] over what he had spent on it, while it had collapsed upon its trellises, and said, "Oh, I wish I had not associated with my Lord anyone." (18:42)
